name: posthog
title: PostHog
description: Open the web app, search PostHog resources, and run read-only analytics with AI tools.
author: david_newell

commands.name: feature-flags
commands.title: Feature Flags
commands.description: Search your feature flags on PostHog.
commands.subtitle: PostHog

commands.name: cohorts
commands.title: Cohorts
commands.description: Search cohorts on PostHog and open them in the web app.
commands.subtitle: PostHog

commands.name: dashboards
commands.title: Dashboards
commands.description: Search dashboards on PostHog and open them in the web app.
commands.subtitle: PostHog

commands.name: persons
commands.title: Search Persons
commands.description: Search persons on PostHog and open their profile.
commands.subtitle: PostHog

preferences.name: personalAPIKey
preferences.title: Personal API Key
preferences.description: Your phx_ personal API key for PostHog. Project keys starting with phc_ are ignored by AI tools.

preferences.name: dataRegionURL
preferences.title: Data Region
preferences.description: The region where your PostHog account is hosted.

preferences.name: defaultProjectId
preferences.title: Default Project ID
preferences.description: Optional project ID to use when AI tools are called without an explicit projectId.

tools.name: whoami
tools.title: Who Am I
tools.description: Verify the PostHog personal API key and return the authenticated user and organization. Use this to debug authentication.

tools.name: list-projects
tools.title: List Projects
tools.description: List PostHog projects with their numeric IDs. Call this before analytics if the project ID is unknown, and pass the chosen projectId to other tools.

tools.name: query-hogql
tools.title: Run HogQL Query
tools.description: Run a read-only HogQL SELECT query against a PostHog project and return compact column-mapped rows. Use this for analytics, funnels, DAU, retention, event counts, cohorts, and metric calculations. Always include a LIMIT for exploratory row-level queries. Prefer aggregate queries for final answers. The tool caps returned rows and truncates long values to avoid bloating context.

tools.name: get-schema
tools.title: Get Schema
tools.description: Fetch the PostHog HogQL database schema with optional table/search filters. Use this to discover tables and columns before writing HogQL. Keep includeColumns false or use table filters when broad schema would be too large.

tools.name: list-events
tools.title: List Events
tools.description: List recent raw events for a PostHog project with optional event name and time filters. Use for sampling and debugging, not for metrics. Request specific propertyKeys to avoid large payloads.

tools.name: list-persons
tools.title: List Persons
tools.description: Search/list PostHog persons for a project. Returns compact person metadata, distinct IDs, and optionally selected properties. Use propertyKeys to avoid large payloads.

tools.name: list-insights
tools.title: List Insights
tools.description: List/search saved PostHog insights for a project. Use this to find existing dashboards or definitions before recreating metrics in HogQL.

tools.name: get-insight
tools.title: Get Insight
tools.description: Get a saved PostHog insight by ID, optionally including filters and results. Use after List Insights when you need the exact metric definition.

tools.name: list-feature-flags
tools.title: List Feature Flags
tools.description: List/search feature flags for a PostHog project. Include filters only when you need rollout or condition details.

ai.instructions: Use PostHog tools for analytics in a context-efficient way. First identify the project with list-projects unless the user provided a projectId or configured a default. Prefer query-hogql for actual numbers and use aggregate SELECTs instead of fetching raw events. Use get-schema only with search or table filters when possible. Use list-events and list-persons for sampling/debugging only, with small limits and propertyKeys. Always pass projectId explicitly once known. Treat all tools as read-only. Never use a phc_ project key for authentication, only a phx_ personal API key. When returning analytics, include the query logic or key filters in prose, but do not dump large raw result sets unless asked.

evals.input: @posthog what projects do I have access to?
@posthog show DAU for the last 7 days in project 123
@posthog what columns are available on events in project 123?
